name: Athlete
summary: A competitor who trains body and mind for organized sport—elevated from amateur Greek games to global professional leagues with sponsorship and broadcast billions.
duties: Follow training plans, compete in events, maintain eligibility, represent sponsors, and recover from injury.
skills: Discipline, sport-specific technique, teamwork, media composure, nutrition awareness.
tools: Sport-specific gear, timing systems, video analysis, recovery equipment.
regions: Olympic circuits, national leagues, collegiate systems worldwide.

events.label: Modern Olympics revival
events.description: Pierre de Coubertin's Athens games relaunch international amateur sport structures.

educationPath: Youth academies, collegiate programs, national team pipelines.
modernSectors: Professional leagues, Olympics, endurance events, action sports.

name: Referee
summary: An on-field official who enforces rules and manages player safety—professionalized as leagues codified standards and video review changed accountability.
duties: Start and stop play, issue penalties, consult assistants, write reports, and manage confrontations.
skills: Rule mastery, positioning, decisiveness, fitness, conflict de-escalation.
tools: Whistle, cards, flags, radio headsets, VAR monitors.
regions: FIFA football, NBA, NFL, rugby unions globally.

events.label: VAR adoption
events.description: Video assistant referees spread from top leagues to major tournaments.

educationPath: Federation certification ladders, fitness tests, seminar rules updates.
modernSectors: Professional leagues, amateur associations, tournament contractors.

name: Sports Agent
summary: A representative who negotiates player contracts, endorsements, and transfers—emerged as free agency and TV money turned athletic talent into major legal and financial assets.
duties: Negotiate deals, market clients, manage brand partnerships, and advise on career moves.
skills: Contract law literacy, networking, persuasion, salary cap rules, ethics.
tools: Agency CRM, contract templates, league CBA documents, data analytics.
regions: NFL, NBA, European football, cricket IPL economies.

events.label: Bosman ruling effects
events.description: EU football labor mobility increases agent leverage in transfer markets.

educationPath: Sports law courses, league agent certification, MBA optional.
modernSectors: Player agencies, marketing boutiques, transfer advisory.

name: Esports Player
summary: A professional competitor in organized video-game tournaments—turned hobbyist LAN parties into salaried roster spots with coaches, analysts, and global streaming audiences.
duties: Scrims, VOD review, tournament travel, stream obligations, and team communications.
skills: Mechanical skill, game sense, teamwork, stress tolerance, brand presence.
tools: Gaming PC, peripherals, team voice comms, analytics dashboards.
regions: South Korea, China, North America, Europe esports hubs.

events.label: League of Legends esports
events.description: Riot's structured league model mainstreames esports careers and franchising.

educationPath: Ranked ladder grinding, academy teams, org tryouts.
modernSectors: Franchised leagues, streaming, brand sponsorship, collegiate esports.

name: Game Designer
summary: A creator who defines rules, levels, and player experiences for tabletop and digital games—professionalized as arcades and home consoles created dedicated design roles.
duties: Write design docs, prototype mechanics, balance economies, playtest, and iterate with engineering and art.
skills: Systems thinking, narrative sense, UX empathy, spreadsheet modeling, collaboration.
tools: Paper prototypes, Unity/Unreal, Miro boards, analytics telemetry.
regions: Silicon Valley studios, Japanese publishers, indie global communities.

events.label: Mobile and indie boom
events.description: App stores and digital distribution lower barriers for small design teams.

educationPath: Game design degrees, modding communities, studio internships.
modernSectors: AAA studios, mobile F2P, tabletop publishers, serious games.

name: Jockey
summary: A lightweight rider who pilots racehorses at flat or jump tracks—occupation shaped by British turf culture and now globalized from Dubai to Hong Kong circuits.
duties: Ride morning works, study form, race tactically, weigh in, and report to trainers.
skills: Balance, race reading, whip regulation compliance, fitness, horsemanship.
tools: Racing silks, saddle, goggles, weighing scales.
regions: UK, Ireland, Kentucky, Japan, Australia racing jurisdictions.

events.label: Parimutuel betting growth
events.description: Legal wagering systems expand purses and professionalize jockey careers.

educationPath: Racing school apprenticeships, exercise rider progression.
modernSectors: Flat racing, jumps, quarter horse, international ship-ins.

name: Sports Commentator
summary: A broadcast voice who narrates live play and supplies analysis—born with radio sports and now split between traditional TV booths and streaming co-commentary.
duties: Research teams, call action, fill dead air, interview guests, and sync with production.
skills: Quick description, storytelling, neutrality or color persona, stat recall.
tools: Headset, monitor, stat feeds, telestrator (TV).
regions: BBC, ESPN, Sky, regional radio sports networks.

events.label: ESPN launch
events.description: 24-hour sports cable multiplies commentator roles and highlight formats.

educationPath: Broadcast journalism, former player credibility, demo reels.
modernSectors: TV, radio, streaming, podcast recap shows.

name: Sports Medicine Physician
summary: A doctor specializing in athletic injuries and performance health—field formalized as team physicians became standard in professional and collegiate sport.
duties: Diagnose injuries, clear return-to-play, coordinate surgery referrals, and advise on load management.
skills: Orthopedics, concussion protocols, communication with coaches, evidence-based rehab.
tools: MRI orders, sideline assessment kits, injection supplies.
regions: Team medical staffs, Olympic delegations, private sports clinics.
originLabel: Team physician roles in pro sport

events.label: ACL reconstruction advances
events.description: Arthroscopic surgery improvements reshape career longevity narratives.

educationPath: MD, family or ortho residency, sports fellowship, team physician certs.
modernSectors: Pro teams, universities, endurance medicine, youth safety programs.

name: Athletic Trainer
summary: A certified practitioner who provides sideline care, taping, and rehab coordination—distinct from personal trainers, embedded in schools and pro locker rooms.
duties: Evaluate acute injuries, implement rehab plans, maintain hydration, and document incidents.
skills: Emergency care, anatomy, communication with physicians, recordkeeping.
tools: Medical kits, ice baths, modalities, EMR systems.
regions: NCAA, NFL, NHS sport partnerships, high school athletics.

events.label: NATA certification growth
events.description: Licensure spreads, distinguishing athletic trainers from fitness coaches.

educationPath: Athletic training degree, BOC exam, state licensure.
modernSectors: Schools, pros, performing arts, industrial onsite programs.

name: Umpire
summary: A baseball or cricket official who judges balls, strikes, and dismissals—title distinct from referee in North American diamond sports and Commonwealth cricket.
duties: Call pitches, rule on plays at bases, manage ejections, and file incident reports.
skills: Rules precision, positioning, crew communication, heat tolerance.
tools: Mask, indicator, brush, radio.
regions: MLB, NPB, ICC member nations.

events.label: Expanded MLB replay
events.description: Manager challenges and replay centers change umpire accountability.

educationPath: Umpire schools, minor league advancement, fitness standards.
modernSectors: Professional baseball, cricket boards, amateur leagues.

name: Professional Golfer
summary: A touring player who earns prize money and endorsements on golf circuits—from Scottish links professionals to modern PGA and LPGA global schedules.
duties: Practice, compete in tournaments, manage travel, fulfill sponsor appearances.
skills: Swing consistency, course management, putting, mental game, etiquette.
tools: Club bag, launch monitor (practice), caddie collaboration, yardage books.
regions: Scotland origins, PGA Tour, DP World Tour, Asian circuits.

events.label: PGA of America founded
events.description: Organized pro tournaments and teaching structures expand golf careers.

educationPath: Junior competition, college golf, Q-school or tour cards.
modernSectors: Tours, teaching pros, course design overlap, senior circuits.

name: Boxer
summary: A fighter who competes in gloved striking bouts under weight classes—codified in Marquess of Queensberry rules and still a major pay-per-view athletic profession.
duties: Train camps, make weight, fight rounds, recover, and manage promotional obligations.
skills: Footwork, punching technique, defense, conditioning, corner trust.
tools: Gloves, wraps, mouthguard, heavy bag, ring.
regions: Global; historic hubs in U.S., Mexico, UK, Philippines.

events.label: Radio fight broadcasts
events.description: National audiences monetize heavyweight bouts and regional gyms.

educationPath: Amateur clubs, Golden Gloves, promoter development deals.
modernSectors: Pro cards, Olympics amateur, fitness boxing crossover.

name: Wrestler
summary: A grappler competing in folkstyle, freestyle, or professional entertainment circuits—ancient Olympic discipline now split between amateur mats and scripted spectacle.
duties: Drill takedowns, compete matches, cut weight, or perform choreographed shows.
skills: Balance, leverage, showmanship (pro), injury awareness.
tools: Singlets, headgear, wrestling shoes, ring ropes (pro).
regions: Iran, Russia, U.S. colleges, Japanese puroresu, WWE.

events.label: Pro wrestling cable boom
events.description: National TV expands entertainment wrestling as distinct career path.

name: Tennis Player
summary: A racket sport competitor on tour or club circuit—professionalized after Open Era allowed prize money and global ranking systems in the 1970s.
duties: Train, travel the tour, play matches, manage endorsements, and peak for Grand Slams.
skills: Stroke production, court movement, tactics, mental resilience.
tools: Rackets, stringing preferences, hawk-eye challenges, fitness team.
regions: Wimbledon tradition, ATP/WTA worldwide.

events.label: Open Era begins
events.description: Amateur-only Grand Slam restrictions end, professionalizing tennis deeply.

name: Cricketer
summary: A bat-and-ball player in multi-day, one-day, or T20 formats—imperial export game now dominated economically by Indian Premier League scale.
duties: Bat, bowl, field, tour internationally, and adhere to board contracts.
skills: Hand-eye coordination, endurance (Test), tactical awareness, teamwork.
tools: Bat, ball, pads, helmets, analytics for opposition.
regions: England, India, Australia, Caribbean, Pakistan.

events.label: IPL launch
events.description: Franchise T20 league transforms player earnings and calendar.

name: Footballer
summary: An association football player—world's most visible athlete role with billion-dollar transfer markets and youth academy pipelines on every continent.
duties: Train, play league and cup matches, represent national team, and maintain commercial image.
skills: Ball control, tactical discipline, fitness, teamwork, set-piece execution.
tools: Boots, GPS vests, video opposition analysis.
regions: Global; Europe's top five leagues as economic center.

events.label: Bosman ruling
events.description: Free agency within EU reshapes club-player power balance.

name: Basketball Player
summary: A five-on-five court competitor whose professional archetype was defined by the NBA and globalized through Olympic Dream Teams and social media highlights.
duties: Run plays, defend, shoot, travel season schedule, and participate in community marketing.
skills: Shooting, passing, lateral quickness, court vision, pick-and-roll reads.
tools: Sneakers, shooting machines, practice facility, wearables.
regions: U.S. NBA epicenter, EuroLeague, growing Africa and Asia leagues.

events.label: Dream Team Barcelona
events.description: NBA stars in Olympics accelerate global basketball popularity.

name: Baseball Player
summary: A diamond sport athlete in pitching, hitting, or fielding roles—rooted in American professional leagues and exported to Japan and Latin America as elite career path.
duties: Game preparation, execute plays, minor-league development, and injury prevention (pitchers).
skills: Hand-eye timing, arm mechanics, situational awareness, clubhouse culture.
tools: Bat, glove, cleats, pitch tracking tech.
regions: MLB, NPB, KBO, Caribbean academies.
originLabel: Organized club baseball in New York

events.label: Jackie Robinson integrates MLB
events.description: Racial integration transforms league talent pool and social significance.

name: Ice Hockey Player
summary: A skater or goaltender in fast ice hockey—Canadian national passion exported to NHL markets in U.S., Nordic countries, and Eastern Europe.
duties: Shift play, check and shoot, goalie crease defense, travel cold-weather schedule.
skills: Skating edges, puck handling, physicality, line chemistry.
tools: Skates, sticks, pads, helmets, video systems.
regions: Canada, U.S. NHL, KHL, Swedish elite leagues.

events.label: NHL formation
events.description: Major professional league structure anchors North American hockey careers.

name: Marathon Runner
summary: An endurance athlete racing 42.195 km—occupation mythologized from Pheidippides legend to Abbott World Marathon Majors prize pools.
duties: Periodized mileage, race strategy, recovery, and sponsor appearances.
skills: Aerobic capacity, pacing discipline, nutrition, injury prevention.
tools: Racing shoes, GPS watch, foam roller, altitude camps.
regions: Boston, London, Berlin, Tokyo, Chicago majors; East African training camps.

events.label: Super shoe era
events.description: Carbon-plated footwear debates reshape records and equipment rules.

name: Cyclist
summary: A bicycle racer in road, track, or mountain disciplines—Tour de France culture turned pedal sport into team-sponsored endurance enterprise.
duties: Train zones, race tactically, maintain bikes, and submit anti-doping controls.
skills: Cadence, drafting, climbing, sprint timing, mechanical basics.
tools: Race bike, power meter, team radio, turbo trainer.
regions: Europe road classics, velodromes, global UCI calendar.

events.label: First Tour de France
events.description: Grand tour model creates cycling's premier professional stage.

name: Swimmer
summary: A pool or open-water racer whose strokes are timed to hundredths of seconds—Olympic showcase sport with year-round club and collegiate pathways.
duties: Lane practices, race meets, dryland training, and meet officiating compliance.
skills: Stroke efficiency, turns, breathing patterns, race pacing.
tools: Goggles, caps, blocks, underwater cameras.
regions: U.S. swim clubs, Australian institutes, European federations.

events.label: Tech suit era
events.description: Polyurethane suits prompt FINA regulation changes after record waves.

name: Gymnast
summary: An acrobatic athlete on apparatus or floor—Soviet and later Chinese systems professionalized rigor now mirrored in NCAA and elite WAG/MAG circuits.
duties: Skill upgrades, routine composition, competition, and strict body maintenance.
skills: Strength, flexibility, air awareness, artistry, fear management.
tools: Apparatus, chalk, crash mats, video review.
regions: Romania, U.S., China, Japan elite programs.

events.label: Perfect 10 spotlight
events.description: Nadia Comăneci score captivates global TV and expands gymnastics popularity.

name: Figure Skater
summary: A performer-athlete who jumps and spins on ice—Winter Olympic glamour role blending athletics, music, and costume design.
duties: Practice elements, compete programs, work with choreographers, maintain boots and blades.
skills: Edge control, jump rotation, performance expression, resilience after falls.
tools: Boots, blades, rink ice time, video replay.
regions: Russia, Canada, Japan, U.S. ISU circuit.

events.label: Scoring reform
events.description: IJS points system replaces 6.0 judging after Salt Lake controversy.

name: Race Car Driver
summary: A motorsport competitor piloting formula, stock, or endurance cars—dangerous spectacle turned engineering team sport with global F1 and NASCAR audiences.
duties: Simulator work, qualify, race, debrief telemetry, and sponsor duties.
skills: Reflexes, spatial awareness, fitness, technical feedback, risk judgment.
tools: Race car, HANS device, helmet, telemetry laptop.
regions: Monaco, Indianapolis, Le Mans, rally stages worldwide.

events.label: F1 World Championship
events.description: Formula One formalizes premier open-wheel professional class.

name: Bullfighter
summary: A matador who performs ceremonial passes and kills in the Spanish corrida—deeply ritualized profession facing animal-welfare bans in many regions.
duties: Train with capes, face bulls in ring, execute faena, and maintain tradition patronage.
skills: Courage, footwork, cape geometry, showmanship, injury recovery.
tools: Suit of lights, muleta, sword, banderillas.
regions: Spain, Mexico, southern France, Peru.

events.label: Catalonia ban
events.description: Regional prohibitions shrink arenas and debate profession's future.

name: Fencing Master
summary: An instructor in swordplay disciplines—descended from duel culture and Olympic foil, épée, and sabre competition with formal pedagogy certificates.
duties: Teach footwork, drill bouts, choreograph safety, and prepare competitors.
skills: Bladework, tactics, refereeing, pedagogy, history.
tools: Mask, jacket, electric scoring box, piste.
regions: France, Italy, Olympic training centers, university clubs.

events.label: Olympic fencing
events.description: Modern Games include fencing as enduring elite amateur/pro pathway.

name: Archery Instructor
summary: A teacher of bow shooting for sport, hunting ethics, or recreation—revived as Olympic precision event and youth camp staple after medieval martial decline.
duties: Fit equipment, teach form, run ranges safely, and score competitions.
skills: Biomechanics, range safety, equipment tuning, calm coaching.
tools: Recurve or compound bows, targets, backstops, clickers.
regions: Korea Olympic centers, U.S. ranges, traditional bowyers.
originLabel: Bowmanship as martial and hunt skill

events.label: Olympic archery return
events.description: Munich Games restore archery as televised target discipline.

name: Sports Journalist
summary: A reporter who covers games, transfers, and scandals—specialized press box culture from newspaper beats to digital breaking push alerts.
duties: Attend events, interview sources, write stories, post social updates, and fact-check.
skills: Fast writing, network sourcing, stats literacy, ethics under team pressure.
tools: Laptop, press credential, camera, Twitter/X, transcription apps.
regions: Global sports desks; league media partnerships.

events.label: ESPN era
events.description: Cable highlights change deadline rhythm and personality-driven coverage.

name: Stadium Usher
summary: An event staffer who seats guests, checks tickets, and manages crowd flow—part of the live-spectacle workforce scaling with megastadium construction.
duties: Scan tickets, direct aisles, enforce bag policies, assist emergencies, and report disturbances.
skills: Customer service, conflict calm, stadium layout knowledge, standing stamina.
tools: Scanner, radio, vest uniform, flashlight.
regions: MLB, NFL, Premier League, Olympic venues.

events.label: Digital ticketing
events.description: Mobile passes change gate workflows and usher fraud-check roles.

name: Ticket Broker
summary: A reseller who buys and sells event seats on secondary markets—legal gray profession shaped by StubHub-era transparency and artist anti-scalping policies.
duties: Source inventory, price dynamically, fulfill orders, and manage chargeback risk.
skills: Market timing, platform algorithms, customer service, compliance navigation.
tools: Exchange APIs, pricing bots, verified fan programs.
regions: U.S. concerts and sports, UK touts, global resale platforms.

events.label: Online resale platforms
events.description: eBay and StubHub legitimize secondary ticket marketplaces at scale.

name: Sports Marketing Manager
summary: A brand strategist who sells sponsorships, ticket packages, and fan engagement—grew as leagues commercialized naming rights and digital content rights.
duties: Pitch sponsors, activate campaigns, manage CRM segments, and measure attribution.
skills: Partnership sales, creative briefing, data analytics, league compliance.
tools: CRM, sponsorship decks, social dashboards, CRM fan tokens.
regions: League offices, agency sports practices, club commercial departments.

events.label: Global brand deals
events.description: Nike-Jordan model expands athlete-brand co-marketing templates.

educationPath: Sports management degrees, league marketing rotations.

name: Equipment Manager
summary: A locker-room logistics specialist who maintains uniforms, gear, and travel trunks—unsung enabler of pro team readiness on road trips.
duties: Laundry, repair gear, pack trunks, track inventory, and prep practice equipment.
skills: Organization, sewing basics, vendor relationships, discretion.
tools: Sewing machine, helmet tools, barcode inventory, travel cases.
regions: MLB clubhouses, NFL, NHL, college athletics.

events.label: Equipment specialization
events.description: Growing staff sizes formalize equipment manager as career track.

name: Racehorse Trainer
summary: A stable manager who conditions thoroughbreds for racing—combining horsemanship, veterinary judgment, and betting-market reputation.
duties: Design workouts, enter races, manage grooms, liaise with owners and vets.
skills: Horse reading, training periodization, regulatory paperwork, owner communication.
tools: Stopwatch, starting gates, vet records, racing forms.
regions: Kentucky, Newmarket, Chantilly, Middle Eastern turf.

events.label: Racing commission regulation
events.description: State racing boards standardize licensing for trainers and medication rules.

name: Stablehand
summary: A barn worker who feeds, mucks, and exercises horses—entry rung of racing and equestrian economies with dawn-shift physical labor.
duties: Muck stalls, groom coats, hot-walk horses, and assist farriers.
skills: Horse handling, stamina, early hours, injury awareness.
tools: Forks, brushes, tack, wheelbarrows.
regions: Racing yards, show barns, polo clubs worldwide.

events.label: Professional racing barns
events.description: Commercial stables employ dedicated grooms at scale beyond estate staff.

name: Bookmaker
summary: An odds-setter who accepts wagers on sporting outcomes—legalized betting shops and mobile apps transformed underground turf accountants into regulated corporations.
duties: Price markets, manage liability, pay winners, comply with AML, and market promotions.
skills: Probability, risk trading, customer acquisition, regulatory compliance.
tools: Trading screens, risk dashboards, shop terminals, mobile apps.
regions: UK betting shops, Las Vegas, Australian TAB, online global operators.

events.label: U.S. sports betting legalization wave
events.description: PASPA repeal opens state-licensed mobile wagering markets.

name: Video Game Tester
summary: A quality-assurance worker who hunts bugs and verifies fixes before game releases—often entry path into studios though glamorized beyond repetitive regression work.
duties: Execute test cases, log defects, reproduce crashes, verify patches, and sign off builds.
skills: Attention to detail, documentation, patience, basic technical literacy.
tools: Bug trackers, dev kits, capture software, test matrices.
regions: Game studios in North America, Japan, Eastern Europe.

events.label: AAA production scale
events.description: Larger teams formalize QA departments and localization testing.

educationPath: QA certificates, studio temp contracts, portfolio bug reports.

name: Board Game Designer
summary: A creator of tabletop rules and components—revived by Kickstarter and convention culture after mid-century TV diverted family game nights.
duties: Prototype mechanics, playtest groups, pitch publishers, and manage art production.
skills: Probability intuition, theme integration, blind testing, Kickstarter marketing.
tools: Cardstock prototypes, meeples, Tabletop Simulator, crowdfunding pages.
regions: Essen Spiel, U.S. designer cons, global Kickstarter backers.

events.label: Kickstarter board boom
events.description: Crowdfunding lets designers bypass traditional publishers.

name: Chess Grandmaster
summary: An elite chess player holding FIDE grandmaster title—ancient court game now supported by engines, streaming, and classical tournament prize funds.
duties: Study openings, compete classical and rapid events, coach students, and create content.
skills: Calculation, pattern memory, endurance, psychological stamina, preparation.
tools: Chess engines, databases, digital boards, streaming setup.
regions: Soviet school tradition, Norway (Carlsen era), global online arenas.

events.label: Fischer-Spassky spotlight
events.description: Cold War match globalizes chess as spectator mind sport.

name: Caddie
summary: A golfer's bag carrier and course advisor who reads greens and manages clubs—Scottish links tradition now including professional tour loops earning percentage of purses.
duties: Carry bag, clean clubs, advise yardages, read greens, and maintain player morale.
skills: Course knowledge, physical fitness, interpersonal tact, math.
tools: Yardage book, towel, rangefinder (where allowed), golf bag.
regions: Scottish links, PGA Tour loops, resort guest caddie programs.

events.label: Caddie association organizing
events.description: Tour caddies negotiate standards and share contract norms.
